properties.Codes_All: Spaces, Historic, Neighbourhood, Navigation, Appreciation, Community, Friendship, Important Figures, Memory, Hopes
properties.Text: the principal did tell me that he had strongly lobbied the school to follow the government direction of schools going independent and moving out to big campuses, which would have been your school. And when you talk to Ms Heng and some of the old girls, we sometimes say, of course because we're the different generation, a bit nostalgic, what if SCGS remained a small boutique school and our dance troupe was very famous? I think it's still very famous, which is good. that would be maybe an argument to say, no, you should go on with the times. But sometimes you look back and we are a bit nostalgic and say, wouldn't it be amazing in Orchard Road if you had retained some of these heritage schools and not followed the national trend where they're big and massive and all that, but just kept it as a boutique girls' school that specialised in dance and the arts and stuff that. We've been very cool. I'm sure it's still a wonderful school. One of our friends, her daughter, went to SCGS, but she said she felt it was very different. But I'm , hey, anyone whose kids are going to the school 30 years later is going to say that it's different. I imagine . But sometimes you look at Orchard Road, you're talking about the soul of a city and everything. Would it not have been lovely to have still had a school here with the students and all that? Instead, it's commercial that everyone says, well, what is Orchard Road about? The same malls, same things in the same malls. And it was in that context that I thought, well, yeah, that would have been cool. And I suppose that's why when we pass it by, we're , oh no, what's going to happen? I'm also realistic enough to know that just making it an art centre, I'm not sure how that works out.

properties.Codes_All: Spaces, Historic, Navigation, Appreciation, Fear, Community, Collaboration, Important Figures, Hopes
properties.Text: But then you have got this structure where you've got rooms and everything. perhaps the government knows what are the... Perhaps the government knows which organisations are looking for rental and which of those causes or cultures or things that they want to keep going, in which case that would be lovely that members of the public could have a space to hang. But if they wanted to go and attend a workshop or they wanted to look at something, it's there and people are using the space functionally. But at the same time, anyone can come and stroll around and hang. I was recently... I just came back from my family vacation and my kids who are in their 20 to 24 range were saying that there's no space in our malls in Singapore to just sit down and hang. Whereas they saw in Sapporo, every mall would have a sizeable area that was beautifully done up with trees and benches and they're just meant for you to go and sit and enjoy. And do you imagine that to be a... That would be cool if you could do that here. It's a living space as well. And you have dance. That would speak something to the heritage of the school that you've got arts groups, dance groups, women's associations and everything. Men's associations would be fine too. In my company, the CEO, the CFO, the Chief Baker, they're all women. But we have fantastic men. when you say that this study is about spaces for women and everything, I think it's good and it's nice because I came from an all-girls school but I never downplay the role. Because I'm , hello, we're 50-50, right? There's no point having one dominant or have special treatment over the other as long as we all live harmoniously and we all bring important values and skills to the experience. But given the history of it as SEGS, it would be nice if there were some causes and things that felt related to the history of the school here.

properties.Codes_All: Design and Architecture, Historic, Appreciation, Memory, Conservation, Hopes
properties.Text: my first home with my husband was a shop house, not a beautiful one with tiles, but just a plain, but it was a pre-war shop house. And we built it before it was conserved. the top floor, we could take off a bit of the roof, made it open air, it was really nice. And then while we were living there, it became conserved. And then I was there for 20 over years. And then we wanted to build a granny room and stuff, but they said, I think I needed to take out a bit of the roof this big. This big. And then I would have retained the air well and all that. They said no, because you'll be touching the original roof.

Instead, they said just shut off the air well, and then follow the template where you have this really skinny block.
